behavior_summary: U8P_CUFFS::look is a short 'U8P_CUFFS::look' (examine) handler: when triggered it emits a single bark with the text "shackle " and then executes a suspend.
There are no menu options, no conditional branches, no global flags read or written, and no calls to external systems or item/NPC APIs in this script.
The function appears to be purely flavor/description tied to the game's inspect/U8P_CUFFS::look action; suspend likely ends or pauses the U8P_CUFFS::look process after the bark.
notable_control_flow: U8P_CUFFS::look: immediate bark (player-facing text) followed by a suspend that terminates/pauses the process.
The implementation contains minimal structure (some Unknown nodes in the decompilation) but no branching or loops — control flow is linear and ends at suspend.
open_questions: Does 'suspend' here fully terminate the U8P_CUFFS::look process or pause it waiting for the engine to resume? (inferred behavior only)
Is U8P_CUFFS::look also invoked by any fast-area entry triggers in addition to player-driven 'U8P_CUFFS::look' actions, or only by explicit examine events?
Is the trailing space in the bark text "shackle " meaningful (formatting artifact) or intended as part of displayed text?
